results.title: 4 Common Imaging Problems (and how to fix them) — NuNano AFM Probes
results.content:
1.

Problem: Unexpected patterns in images

Cause: Tip artefacts

Structures appearing duplicated or irregular shaped features repeating across the image can indicate a broken tip or contamination on the tip. With a blunt tip, structures will appear larger and trenches will appear smaller. If you are seeing either of those things try using a new probe to see if that makes the problem disappear. At NuNano we understand this frustration first hand! We inspect every probe to guarantee tip sharpness, to ensure no contaminated or broken probes which could produce unexpected shapes.

AFM Image showing duplicate features, taken from: Lamentation of Arti(e)facts - 2020 - Wiley Analytical Science [...] James Vicary

Tips and Tricks

# 4 Common Imaging Problems (and how to fix them)

James Vicary

Tips and Tricks

How frustrating is it to have spent time carefully preparing your sample and setting up the AFM only to find that you’re not getting the images you expected?

Having problems acquiring accurate images is incredibly common, especially if working with the AFM is reasonably new to you.

In most instances it will be due to one of three main things - your sample, the probe you’re using, or because someone else has fiddled with the AFM default settings.

To help navigate some of the common causes of imaging problems you may come across we’ve compiled a short and simple guide of the main problems, what causes them and how you can fix them.

1.

Problem: Unexpected patterns in images [...] In many cases electrical noise can be out of your control, since it’s likely governed by the quality of the electrical circuits in the building, other instrumentation etc. Sometimes it’s possible to identify periods (e.g. early mornings/late evenings) when this problem doesn’t persist, albeit necessitating a change in working habits…)

Cause B: Interference of the laser reflecting off the sample.

results.title: How to fix my Microsoft Photos app not opening on my Windows 10 PC when I click it? - Microsoft Q&A
results.content:
' cx='32' cy='32' r='32' /%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' dominant-baseline='middle' text-anchor='middle' fill='%23FFF' %3EWH%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Exactly the same issue for me. Very frustrating

' cx='32' cy='32' r='32' /%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' dominant-baseline='middle' text-anchor='middle' fill='%23FFF' %3EK%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E)

I am having the same issue, can Microsoft please clarify cause of issue and advise on a fix?

' cx='32' cy='32' r='32' /%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' dominant-baseline='middle' text-anchor='middle' fill='%23FFF' %3ESH%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E) [...] Same thing is happening to me. I can open my pictures on things like Microsoft Paint, Adobe Animate, etc. However no matter what I do I can't get microsoft photos to open. Ive tried resetting, reinstalling, using the command prompt reinstall technique, but It simply won't open. Please help! This is very frustrating and I've tried every technique I can find online. This is day 3 for me.

' cx='32' cy='32' r='32' /%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' dominant-baseline='middle' text-anchor='middle' fill='%23FFF' %3EM%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E) [...] It would appear the app is temporarily broken due to issues with the app package. Some users on other forums reported functionality returning by uninstalling and installing an older package. You are not the only one struggling, but the unfortunate reality is that the only thing we as consumers have the power to do in this contract is wait.

' cx='32' cy='32' r='32' /%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' dominant-baseline='middle' text-anchor='middle' fill='%23FFF' %3EG%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E)

I am having the exact same issue with my Photos and Phone Link apps. They both randomly stopped working recently for no apparent reason. Sounds like this is affecting multiple people though. So, hopefully Microsoft is aware and working on a fix.

results.title: Review Scores Are Broken: How They Hurt Games More Than They Help
results.content:
and publishers a voice, but it's to help the consumer as well. Because your money is precious. you work very hard for it and you want to spend it in the right places. We're trying to direct you towards games that we think you'll enjoy and maybe bring an understanding as to why you might not enjoy those games, too. And of course, it's important when you look at a review to understand that I'm just one individual person. My opinion doesn't stand for the rest of the world. There have been games that I've not really enjoyed very much that other people have had a blast playing. And I say congratulations. I'll comment to them below. you know, I'm glad that you got on with this one. I didn't, but hey, it's just an opinion. It's to help guide people. And more often than not, we end up hearing [...] 39 comments
### Transcript: [...] if you tend to go towards the indie Krampus side of things. But, you know, I really hate to put all of this effort and time and energy into something and then have people not listen to the actual words you said. So many times we get comments on our videos, asking questions that are clearly answered in the video. So, I think attention spans in a lot of ways are dying, but maybe that's just the way it's always been. There's another problem here, too, and that's that a seven can still mean good depending on the outlet, but it's dismissed often by audiences. They see that seven and they see, gosh, what was that in school? A C minus. You know, I wasn't super happy to get a C minus back in the day. I strive to do better on my next paper. And I think that mentality still exists in a lot of
